<!-- generated by claude-dev-kit: edit .claude/kit.overlay/skills/plan-consensus/SKILL.md, not this file -->

# plan-consensus â€” stress-test a plan to consensus, as a DAG (not a chat)

Before you finalize a plan, run it through the `critic` agent in a **bounded loop**. This exists
because the main thread, having just written the plan, has already fallen for it â€” it cannot be its
own critic. A **cold fork** can. Model the loop as a directed pipeline, never a back-and-forth
conversation (a chat lets both sides "meet in the middle" in a sycophantic truce).

## The loop

1. **Synthesis** â€” you (the main thread = the planner) write/update the plan file.
2. **Destruction (cold fork)** â€” spawn the `critic` agent on the current plan (locally:
   `bin/kit-critic <plan-file> [prior-findings-file]`, or via the Agent tool with
   `subagent_type: critic`). Its critique is an **immutable artifact** â€” a static report, NOT a
   partner to argue with. Each finding carries a **critic-owned severity**
   (`bug`/`robustness`/`tradeoff`/`style`) AND a **stable ID** (`F1`, `F2`, â€¦).
   - **Carry findings across the cold forks (round â‰¥ 2).** The critic is stateless â€” a fresh fork has
     no memory of the previous round, so a real finding can *stochastically disappear* (round 2 just
     didn't look there). To stop that false-negative, feed every round â‰¥ 2 the **open findings from
     the prior round** as a `[PRIOR FINDINGS]` block. The critic must then OPEN its report with a
     `## Prior findings ruling` section that rules each prior ID explicitly: **open / fixed /
     withdrawn**, with proof. A prior `bug`/`robustness` ID stays **open** until a critic *explicitly*
     clears it â€” it cannot vanish by silence. Any ID it rules `open` it must **restate as a full `##`
     section** (id + body), so the finding's substance â€” not just its number â€” survives the next cold
     fork (else it dies by *amnesia*: a bodyless ID gets withdrawn "for lack of substance").
   - **The carry-file is machine-DERIVED, never hand-curated** (else the planner â€” the conflicted
     actor â€” just drops an inconvenient ID and the disappearance returns). `bin/kit-critic` saves each
     report to `.claude/.critic-report.md`; derive the next round's prior-findings file from it:
     `python3 <kit>/lib/critic-prior.py .claude/.critic-report.md > .claude/.critic-prior.md`, then
     `bin/kit-critic <plan> .claude/.critic-prior.md`. Dropping an ID now means editing a generated
     file â€” visible in the human's diff. If you re-fork WITHOUT carrying findings while a recent
     critique exists, `bin/kit-critic` WARNs (the virgin-round bypass is visible, not silent); pass
     `--fresh` to confirm a deliberate clean start. (Residual: a planner who *edits the generated file*
     or always passes `--fresh` to dodge the carry is out of scope â€” that is the human firewall's job,
     the same as `--no-verify`. The mechanism makes the honest path the easy one; it cannot force it.)
3. **Resolution** â€” for EACH finding, pick exactly one outcome:
   - **Fix** â†’ change the plan to remove the cause; or
   - **Accept** â†’ move it to a `## Accepted operational risks (trade-offs)` section in the plan, with
     a one-line rationale.
   **The rule that keeps the registry honest:** you may Accept ONLY findings the critic labelled
   `tradeoff` or `style`. A `bug` or unguarded `robustness` finding **cannot be moved to the
   registry** â€” it must be Fixed, or the loop escalates to the human. You may NOT downgrade the
   critic's severity; only the cold critic sets it. Each registry line keeps the critic's original
   severity, so a mis-filed finding is visible.

## Termination (a real fixpoint, not a dice cap)

The loop ends when, **in the latest round**, ALL of:
- every prior `bug`/`robustness` finding ID is ruled `fixed` or `withdrawn` in `## Prior findings
  ruling` (explicitly cleared â€” never merely absent), AND
- no NEW `bug`/unguarded-`robustness` finding is raised, AND
- every remaining finding is Fixed or legitimately Accepted into the registry.

This is the fixpoint the carry-forward buys: because a prior blocking ID cannot disappear by silence,
"clean" means *explicitly cleared*, not *not-re-rolled*. **Cap = 3 critic rounds** remains, but only as
a runaway safety valve â€” termination is the explicit-clear condition above, not the cap. After a Fix,
**re-fork on the revised plan** (with the `[PRIOR FINDINGS]` block) so the fix is itself validated.
If the cap is hit with any prior `bug`/`robustness` ID still `open`: **STOP. Hand the open list + the
registry to the human. Never auto-accept to escape the loop.**

## Token economy (don't re-bill a round that can't change the verdict)

A cold fork re-pays the whole plan as input every round â€” that is the cost of independence, so spend
rounds only where they can move the verdict:

- **No re-fork without a change to validate.** Re-fork ONLY after a **Fix** (a plan edit the next round
  must check) or a genuinely **new open blocker** to carry. If the latest round made zero Fixes â€”
  everything was legitimately Accepted as `tradeoff`/`style` and raised no new `bug`/`robustness` â€” you
  are already at the fixpoint; spawning another round just re-bills an identical critique. Stop.
- **Carry only OPEN blockers, machine-derived.** `lib/critic-prior.py` already emits *exactly* the
  unresolved `bug`/`robustness` IDs (fixed/withdrawn dropped) â€” so `[PRIOR FINDINGS]` stays minimal and
  the planner can't pad it. Don't hand-append resolved findings "for context".
- **Terse is cheaper twice.** The critic's terse contract (â‰¤3-line bodies, `path:line` not prose) shrinks
  both the round's output bill AND the `[PRIOR FINDINGS]` block re-fed next round.
- **Round â‰¥ 2: feed the DELTA, not the whole plan.** A round Nâˆ’1â†’N plan revision IS a delta case â€” the
  prior plan is the baseline, the revised plan is the change. Instead of re-feeding the whole plan to the
  cold critic, feed a `[DELTA]` block (diff of prior-vs-revised plan â†’ changed ranges + hunks) alongside
  `[PRIOR FINDINGS]`; the `critic` agent's `[DELTA]` mode reads only the changed sections and escalates
  `needs-full-doc` rather than false-pass. **`bin/kit-critic --delta <prior-round-plan> <revised-plan>
  [prior-findings]` does this for you** â€” it diffs the two plan files, derives the changed ranges, sends
  the critic the path+ranges+hunks (not the plan body), and still writes the program-attested consensus
  marker + keeps the model fallback. So keep each round's plan as its own file to serve as the next
  round's baseline. See the **`critique-delta` skill** for the caller-side steps
  and the honesty guard. Caveat for plans: a plan may have no commit of its own, so keep the **prior-round
  plan file** as the explicit baseline. The local-correctness axis matters MORE for plans â€” a revised
  step can be load-bearing-wrong without contradicting anything â€” so the delta audits BOTH axes, not just
  contradictions. For a recall-sensitive plan, run k cold deltas and union (see `critique-delta`).

This is the in-loop slice. The remaining levers (partition into parallel section-critics; cache the
static prefix) are catalogued in `docs/cold-critic-token-economy.md` with their live/deferred/unverified
status.

## Human firewall

The human reviews a **short diff** â€” *what you Fixed in response to the critic* + *the Accepted-risks
registry with each finding's critic severity* â€” not hundreds of lines of code. Their approval is the
go signal.

## Record consensus (so the ExitPlanMode guard stays quiet)

You do **not** record this by hand. `bin/kit-critic` writes `.claude/.plan-consensus` itself on every
real critic run, stamping it with the plan's hash â€” so the marker is **program-attested**, not a
model-typed `touch`. The `plan-consensus-guard.sh` hook checks the marker's freshness on ExitPlanMode
and only WARNs (never blocks) if it is missing, stale, or unsigned (a bare `touch` has no `plan-hash:`
line and no longer counts â€” running the loop is now the laziest way to a valid marker). Honest limit:
this attests "the critic ran recently on a plan", not "the loop reached its fixpoint" â€” that discipline
(the termination condition above) stays yours and the human's. If you fork the critic via the Agent
tool (`subagent_type: critic`) instead of `bin/kit-critic`, no marker is written and the guard will warn
â€” prefer `bin/kit-critic` so the record happens for free.

## Feeding the critic in THIS project (animata-pm)

Plans under consensus live at **`.claude/plans/<name>.md`** (e.g. `.claude/plans/substrate.md`). Model
and constraints come from `.claude/kit.config.sh` (`KIT_CRITIC_MODEL=opus`, `KIT_PLANNER_MODEL=opus`,
`KIT_CRITIC_CONSTRAINTS`). The launcher is the kit's **`.claude-dev-kit/bin/kit-critic`** (subscription
session, $0) â€” NOT `pm/bin/code-review` (that is the separate PR-vs-Ð¢Ð— gate run by `code-critic` after a
PR exists; this loop runs on the *plan*, before any code).

**Round snapshots = the delta baseline.** A plan has no commit of its own mid-loop, so keep each
critiqued round's text as an explicit baseline file under `.claude/plans/.consensus/` (gitignored,
transient). Snapshot the plan *right after* each round's critique, so the baseline is exactly the text
that was critiqued.

    mkdir -p .claude/plans/.consensus

**Round 1 â€” full cold critique** (you need the baseline):

    .claude-dev-kit/bin/kit-critic .claude/plans/<name>.md
    cp .claude/plans/<name>.md .claude/plans/.consensus/<name>.r1.md      # snapshot what was critiqued

Resolve every finding (Fix or Accept), editing `.claude/plans/<name>.md`. Then machine-derive the carry:

    python3 .claude-dev-kit/lib/critic-prior.py .claude/.critic-report.md > .claude/.critic-prior.md

**Round â‰¥2 â€” feed the DELTA, not the whole plan** (see the `critique-delta` skill for the contract):

    .claude-dev-kit/bin/kit-critic --delta .claude/plans/.consensus/<name>.r$((N-1)).md \
        .claude/plans/<name>.md .claude/.critic-prior.md
    cp .claude/plans/<name>.md .claude/plans/.consensus/<name>.r$N.md     # re-anchor for the next round

`--delta` diffs the prior-round snapshot against the current plan and sends the critic only the changed
ranges + hunks (it Reads the enclosing sections itself), while still writing the program-attested
`.plan-consensus` marker and keeping the opus fallback. If the plan is identical to the snapshot there is
no change to validate â‡’ `kit-critic` exits 0 (you are already at the fixpoint). The report always lands
at `.claude/.critic-report.md`. For a recall-sensitive plan, run kâ‰¥2 deltas and union their findings.

NOTE: `KIT_CRITIC_CONSTRAINTS` in `.claude/kit.config.sh` currently holds the generic kit default â€” set it
to the animata-pm plan envelope (sim determinism / lane ownership / golden-touch / Ð¢Ð— acceptance) when a
plan's survival depends on those, so the cold critic stress-tests against the real constraints.
